skill.name: latex-posters
skill.description: Creates academic-poster writing packages for LaTeX using beamerposter, tikzposter, or baposter. Use when a user needs poster-ready section copy, figure plans, captions, and package-specific layout decisions for conference or thesis posters.
skill.summary: Generate publication-ready LaTeX academic posters with writing packages, figure plans, and layout guidance for conferences and thesis defenses.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 543 static analyzer findings are false positives caused by pattern matching on LaTeX color codes, markdown backticks, and documentation references in .tex and .md files. The only executable code is a benign PDF quality inspection shell script (scripts/review_poster.sh) that runs standard system utilities (pdfinfo, pdffonts, pdfimages) with no network calls, no obfuscation, and no malicious behavior. Hardcoded URLs point only to the source GitHub repository. No prompt injection, no data exfiltration, and no malicious intent detected.

low_findings.title: Shell Script Execution Capability
low_findings.description: The skill bundles a bash script (scripts/review_poster.sh) that executes system commands for PDF quality inspection. The script runs standard utilities (pdfinfo, pdffonts, pdfimages, ls, awk) with no network access or data exfiltration. All command arguments derive from a user-supplied file path, which the script validates for existence but does not sanitize against path traversal. The risk is low because the skill instructs Claude to use this script locally for poster review, and the user controls the input file.

low_findings.title: Documentation URLs Point to External Repository
low_findings.description: Several hardcoded URLs in SKILL.md and reference documentation link to the source GitHub repository. These are legitimate documentation references, not exfiltration endpoints. The URLs are static and do not accept user data.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm in LaTeX/Documentation Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 184 lines across .tex, .md, and .json files as 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' (HIGH/blocker). These are all false positives. The scanner matches patterns like 'MD' followed by digits, 'SHA', or similar substrings that appear in LaTeX color specifications (e.g., 'red!40!blue'), markdown numbering, checksum references, and JSON structure. No actual cryptographic operations exist in these academic writing templates and reference documents.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: Ruby/Shell Backtick Execution in Markdown Files
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged 339 locations across .md files as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are all markdown backtick characters used for inline code formatting and code blocks in documentation. No actual shell or Ruby execution occurs - these are purely documentation formatting syntax.

dangerous_patterns.title: Static Analyzer False Positive: System/Network Reconnaissance in Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged references to system commands and tool names in markdown documentation as 'System reconnaissance' and 'Network reconnaissance'. These are legitimate documentation references to LaTeX compilation tools (pdflatex), system utilities, and package names. No actual reconnaissance code exists.

content.user_title: Create Academic Research Posters with LaTeX
content.value_statement: Writing a conference or thesis poster from a full paper is hard. This skill compresses your research into poster-ready section copy, figure plans, and LaTeX layout decisions for beamerposter, tikzposter, or baposter.
actual_capabilities: Choose the best LaTeX poster package (beamerposter, tikzposter, or baposter) for your use case
Compress a full research paper into a readable poster narrative with section-level word budgets
Create a figure plan where each figure communicates one clear message with caption guidance
Generate poster-ready copy for title, introduction, methods, results, and conclusion sections
Apply A0 poster constraints: 300-800 words max, 5-6 content blocks, body text at 24pt+
Refuse impossible density requests and propose reduced poster architectures
limitations: Only supports LaTeX-based poster workflows - not PowerPoint, Canva, or other visual design tools
Cannot generate or edit actual figure images or graphics files
Word budgets and section plans are recommendations that need author review before final submission
Requires the user to provide source material (paper, abstract, or slide deck) to work from

use_cases.title: Graduate Student Preparing a Conference Poster
use_cases.description: A PhD student needs to turn their accepted paper into an A0 portrait poster. The skill selects tikzposter, provides a section map with word limits, and writes poster-ready copy for each block.

use_cases.title: Research Group Creating a Thesis Defense Poster
use_cases.description: A research group needs a formal poster summarizing their multi-year project for a thesis defense. The skill recommends beamerposter for classic academic aesthetics and structures the content across 5 clear blocks.

use_cases.title: Conference Organizer Standardizing Poster Submissions
use_cases.description: A conference chair wants to provide authors with a standardized poster template and content guidelines. The skill delivers a reusable brief template with quality checklist and package-specific LaTeX assets.
use_cases.target_user: Conference organizers and academic event planners

prompt_templates.title: Basic Poster from Paper Abstract
prompt_templates.prompt: I have a research paper abstract. Help me create an A0 conference poster plan. My abstract: [paste abstract]. The conference is [name], audience is [specialists/general]. I need a poster brief with recommended package, section order, and word budget.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has an abstract and needs a complete poster plan

prompt_templates.title: Full Paper to Poster Compression
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this research paper into a poster-ready writing package. Paper topic: [topic]. Key finding: [one-sentence result]. I need a beamerposter layout with title, intro, methods, 3 results bullets, conclusion, and figure plan. The poster is A0 landscape for a medical conference.
prompt_templates.scenario: User has a full paper and wants a compressed poster narrative

prompt_templates.title: Package Selection Help
prompt_templates.prompt: I am making a research poster for [event type: conference/thesis defense]. My content is [description of content type: heavy on figures / text-heavy / lots of sections]. Which LaTeX package should I use: beamerposter, tikzposter, or baposter? Compare the options for my use case and recommend the best one.
prompt_templates.scenario: User is unsure which LaTeX package fits their needs

prompt_templates.title: Full Poster Writing Package with Figure Planning
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a complete poster writing package from this research: [paste paper sections]. Poster size: A0 portrait. Audience: [specialty] researchers. I need: (1) package recommendation, (2) section map with word budget per section, (3) poster-ready copy for all sections, (4) figure plan with one message per figure and caption guidance, (5) LaTeX template recommendation from assets/, (6) final quality checklist. If my content is too dense for A0, tell me and propose cuts.
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced users who want a complete, production-ready poster package with all deliverables

output_examples.input: Help me make an A0 poster from my paper about deep learning for medical image segmentation. The key finding is 94% accuracy on tumor detection.
output: Poster Brief: A0 Portrait | tikzposter | Medical AI Conference Audience
Section Map: Title → Problem Statement → Methods Summary → Results (3 bullets) → Conclusion → Contact/QR
Word Budget: Title 12 words | Problem 40 words | Methods 60 words | Results 80 words | Conclusion 35 words
Figure Plan: 3 figures — (1) Architecture diagram, (2) ROC curve comparison, (3) Example segmentation outputs
Caption Guidance: Each caption states what the figure proves, not what it shows

output_examples.input: I need a poster for my PhD thesis defense. My project covers 4 studies over 5 years. The audience includes my committee and department.
output: Poster Brief: A0 Landscape | beamerposter | Thesis Defense Audience (committee + department)
Section Map: Title/Author → Central Research Question → Study Overview Timeline → Key Findings (one per study) → Synthesis/Contribution → Future Directions → Acknowledgments
Word Budget: Title 15 words | Question 25 words | Timeline 40 words | Findings 100 words total | Synthesis 50 words | Future 30 words
Figure Plan: 1 timeline figure + 1 key-result highlight figure (max 2 figures for overview poster)
Refusal Note: 4-study detail is too dense for a single poster. Proposing overview-level synthesis rather than per-study detail. Recommend supplementary handouts for study-level data.

best_practices: Always confirm poster size, orientation, audience, and source material before generating any output
Default to tikzposter for modern posters, beamerposter for formal academic settings, and baposter for heavily modular content
Enforce A0 constraints strictly: refuse dense-text requests and propose reduced architectures with clear reasoning
anti_patterns: Do not accept tiny-font dense content requests - posters must remain readable from 2-3 feet away
Do not recommend PowerPoint or non-LaTeX workflows - this skill is LaTeX-only and should state that boundary clearly
Do not fabricate results, metrics, or figure descriptions - all content must be grounded in the user's source material

faq.question: Which LaTeX package should I use for my poster?
faq.answer: Use tikzposter for modern, colorful conference posters. Use beamerposter for formal thesis-defense or traditional academic posters. Use baposter when you need precise multi-column box-based layout control.

faq.question: How many words should an A0 poster contain?
faq.answer: An A0 poster should contain 300 to 800 words total. Keep body text at a minimum of 24pt for readability from 2 to 3 feet away. Use short declarative statements, not dense paragraphs.

faq.question: Can this skill create my figures and graphics?
faq.answer: No. This skill plans figure placement, assigns one message per figure, and writes captions. You must provide or create the actual figure images yourself.

faq.question: What if my research paper is too long for a poster?
faq.answer: The skill will identify density problems and propose cuts. Focus on your one central message, one methods summary, your 2 to 3 strongest results, and one clear conclusion. Do not try to preserve every subsection.

faq.question: Does this skill work with PowerPoint or Canva?
faq.answer: No. This skill works only with LaTeX-based poster workflows using beamerposter, tikzposter, or baposter. It cannot generate PowerPoint or other visual design tool output.

faq.question: How do I use the bundled LaTeX templates?
faq.answer: The skill references templates in the assets directory: beamerposter_template.tex, tikzposter_template.tex, and baposter_template.tex. The skill will recommend which template to use and provide content that fits the template structure.
